values: Moderate

evidence.quote_or_summary: Moderate

values: Suitable for Fit Beginners

evidence.quote_or_summary: Suitable for Fit Beginners

values: Uneven trails with daily ascents and descents
River crossings (gushing streams, Rani Nala up to waist-deep)
Bouldery moraine sections and multiple ledges/ridges near Hampta Pass
Slushy and slippery forest trails in heavy rain
Snow-covered trail sections even in June

evidence.quote_or_summary: Every day will include ascents and descents on uneven trails. The river crossings on this trek can be especially challenging.
You traverse multiple ledges and ridges, trekking continuously on bouldery moraine sections.
In heavy rain, this trail can get slushy and slippery.
You can expect a lot of snow here, even in June.

values: Final ascent from Balu ka Ghera to Hampta Pass is challenging
Descent from Hampta Pass is extremely steep with many switchbacks

evidence.quote_or_summary: The final day of ascent from Balu ka Ghera to Hampta Pass is challenging.
The descent from Hampta Pass is extremely steep and has a lot of switchbacks.

values: Micro spikes, gaiters, helmets, and ropes provided by Indiahikes where required
Wooden log crossings over streams
Snow bridges may be used in June and July
Wooden bridges placed over most streams near Chhatru

evidence.quote_or_summary: our Trek Leaders and Technical Guides will provide you with all necessary guidance, support, and technical gear such as micro spikes, gaiters, helmets, ropes, etc. wherever required.
in June and July, there may be snow bridges on which you can walk across the streams.
you’ll find wooden bridges placed over most of them.

values: Trek reaches 14,065 ft; AMS risk above 10,000 ft
AMS can affect first-time and experienced trekkers alike
Fitness does not eliminate risk if ascent is too fast

evidence.quote_or_summary: The Hampta Pass trek is situated at 14,065 ft above sea level. At altitudes above 10,000 ft, the chances of being hit by Acute Mountain Sickness are real.
Altitude sickness can happen to anybody, first-time trekkers as well as experienced trekkers.
even a fit person climbing too high, too soon runs an equal risk of developing AMS.

values: Prolonged rainfall or snowfall can change trek dynamics
June snow on Balu Ka Ghera trail makes it slippery
July–August rain makes trails slippery and increases river crossing force
Team may turn back from any campsite if weather deteriorates

evidence.quote_or_summary: Rainfall or snowfall, lasting for hours, can change the entire trek dynamic.
In June, when there is a lot of snow on the trail starting from Balu Ka Ghera, the trail can get slippery.
In July and August, rain is a common occurrence. It makes the trail slippery and tricky. Moreover, the force of water at river crossings can increase, making it challenging to cross them.
If the weather deteriorates, the team may need to turn back from any campsite to ensure the safety of all trekkers.

values: High-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers carried by Indiahikes
Closest hospital: Mission Hospital, Manali
Alternate hospital: Manu Diagnostics Center and Hospital, Manali
Trekkers must disclose pre-existing medical conditions to Trek Leader

evidence.quote_or_summary: The Indiahikes team is equipped with high-altitude medical kits, oxygen cylinders, and stretchers to handle any altitude-related emergencies.
Mission Hospital in Manali is the closest one to Hampta Pass, in case of a medical emergency during the trek. Manu Diagnostics Center and Hospital, also in Manali, is another option.
Please inform your Trek Leader about any pre-existing medical conditions or health concerns, no matter how minor they may seem.

values: Pass-crossing trek with limited exit points
If pass not crossed, descend via Jobra to Manali
After crossing pass, must reach Chhatru before going to Manali via Rohtang Pass
Trekkers must actively follow safety protocols

evidence.quote_or_summary: Hampta Pass, being a Pass-crossing trek, has limited exit points.
If you haven’t crossed the pass, then head down Jobra before moving on to Manali.
After crossing the pass, you’ll have to make it to Chhatru before heading to Manali via Rohtang Pass.
safety is a shared responsibility. We need your active participation and attention to safety protocols to ensure a safe and successful trek.
